babysBreath.name: Baby's Breath
babysBreath.description: Early-blooming plants are covered with doubled flowers. Lovely fresh or dried. Perennial, zones 4-8

bachelorsButtonsBB.name: Bachelor's Buttons
bachelorsButtonsBB.description: Exquisite, fragrant, well-doubled flowers bloom from early summer until frost. Great for cutting and borders.

bachelorsButtonsPDM.name: Bachelor's Buttons
bachelorsButtonsPDM.description: This compact annual brings vibrant color and scads of pollinators to your early season garden. Lacy blossoms in shades of blue, pink, purple, red, and white make terrific cutting flowers.

bachelorsButtonsTDMC.name: Bachelor's Buttons
bachelorsButtonsTDMC.description: Glowing mixture of double flowers makes this a fine selection for borders and bouquets.

calendulaPBB.name: Calendula
calendulaPBB.description: So many uses, it's like growing gold in your garden! Edible, pollinator-attracting flowers bloom for months and make long-lasting cut flowers.

canterburyBells.name: Canterbury Bells
canterburyBells.description: Striking plants with unsual flowers in a blend of four vibrant colors. Biennial.

catnip.name: Catnip
catnip.description: Cats love to smell and rub leaves. Sew dried leaves into cat toys. Perennial, zones 4-9.

chicoryW.name: Chicory
chicoryW.description: Also called Blue Dandelion, this dainty wildflower has been used for nutritional and medicinal purposes since the days of ancient Egypt! A hardy, sun-loving biennial favored by pollinators and loved as a wildflower throughout North America.

coreopsisPC.name: Coreopsis
coreopsisPC.description: This prairie wildflower sports sunny yellow blooms with mahogany centers that call out to pollinators. Blooms and delicate foilage bring movement to beds and bouquets.

coreopsisES.name: Coreopsis
coreopsisES.description: First perennial coreopsis to bloom the first year from seed! Perennial, zones 3-9.

cosmosBLMC.name: Cosmos
cosmosBLMC.description: Easy to grow and brightly colored mix of early-flowering, semi-double blooms in yellow and orange.

echinacea.name: Echinacea
echinacea.description: Large, long-lasting rosy purple flowers surround an orange-brown center. Perennial, zones 3-10.

feverfew.name: Feverfew
feverfew.description: Charming clusters of small, daisy shaped flowers on aromatic, bushy plants. Long-blooming, easy to grow and a lovely cut flower.

flax.name: Flax
flax.description: This striking springtime perennial boasts vivid blue blossoms that bloom atop 12-24” tall stems. The sturdy and sun-loving Blue Flax tolerates summer heat, poor soil, and even drought conditions. Easy to grow.

fourOClocksCGMC.name: Four O'Clocks
fourOClocksCGMC.description: Fast growing plants bear fragrant, trumpet-shaped flowers that open each afternoon.

fourOClocksKMC.name: Four O'Clocks
fourOClocksKMC.description: Unique multicolored blooms from mid-day to evening. Drought tolerant.

gazania.name: Gazania
gazania.description: Boldy colored daisy-like flowers open by day, close at night. Blooms in summer and thrives in the dry heat.

lemonBalm.name: Lemon Balm
lemonBalm.description: Lemon scented leaves and sprigs used for tea or flavoring cool drinks. Perennial, zones 4-10.

loveInAMist.name: Love-In-A-Mist
loveInAMist.description: Delightful flowers float atop a mist of lacy foilage. The dried seed pods are papery and quite unusual. Great cut flower. Drought tolerant.

mintKL.name: Mint
mintKL.description: Native to China and Korea, this perennial mint has amazing licorice flavor, tall stature, and powerhouse blue flowers for pollinators. For best flavor, harvest leaves before flowering begins. Grows in clumps and does not spread like other mints. Valued for medicinal properties.

paintedDaisyGMC.name: Painted Daisy
paintedDaisyGMC.description: Daisy-type flowers are great for cutting. Blooms second season. Perannial, zones 5-9.

pansySGB.name: Pansy
pansySGB.description: Fantastic 2-2.5 inch blooms in a rainbow of colors. These cheerful, edible flowers are one of the first in spring and last in fall. Add to a bouquet!

phloxTPM.name: Phlox
phloxTPM.description: Fragrant, tubular florets bloom in vibrant clusters atop tall stems, creating a stunning display perfect for cottage garden enthusiasts. Pollinator favorites.

rudbeckiaBESSMC.name: Rudbeckia
rudbeckiaBESSMC.subName: Black Eyed Susan Single Mixed Colors
rudbeckiaBESSMC.description: 5 inch daisy-like flowers in yellow mahogany and bicolors on long stems. Perennial, zones 6-10.

scabiosaBK.name: Scabiosa
scabiosaBK.description: Historically, scabiosa symbolizes sorrow and lost love, but this stunning variety will bring joy to your pollinators and unique elegance to your garden. Black Knight's wispy, color-rich blossoms are a deep, blackish purple especially lovely in summertime bouquets.

shastaDaisyA.name: Shasta Daisy
shastaDaisyA.description: One of the best Shasta daisies around. Single, white 3 inch blooms. Perennial, zones 4-9.

snapdragon.name: Snapdragon
snapdragon.description: Vibrant mixture in a wide range of colors. Great in beds, borders, and for cutting.

strawflower.name: Strawflower
strawflower.description: Enchanting dwarf strawflower mix is perfect for cut or dried flowers. Tolerates poor soil, heat or drought.

sunflower.name: Sunflower
sunflower.description: Golden yellow, fully double 6 inch blooms great for mid borders, containers and bouquets.

sweetPeaB.name: Sweet Pea
sweetPeaB.description: A luxurious sweet pea, this highly-perfumed Spencer-type beauty brings dramatic color and large, ruffled flowers to the garden and cut-flower bouquets.

sweetPeaHS.name: Sweet Pea
sweetPeaHS.description: The most intensely fragrant sweet pea! Perfume your garden and floral bouquets with intoxicating aromas of these lovely ruffled blossoms.

tomatoSM.name: Tomato
tomatoSM.description: An Italian favorite beloved for its thick, meaty flesh. Perfect for sauces, canning and drying. Indeterminate.

yarrowR.name: Yarrow
yarrowR.description: This perennial sports sprigs of fine crimson florets sprouting from stems covered in soft, fern-like leaves. Expect the fragrant Red Yarrow to bloom in early summer and enjoy its color year-round when dried. Easy to grow and adaptable.

zinniaBBTMC.name: Zinnia
zinniaBBTMC.description: Huge dahlia-flowered blooms, up to 6 inch across, in a blend of all the best zinnia colors.

zinniaCCAMC.name: Zinnia
zinniaCCAMC.subName: Cut and Come Again Mixed Colors
zinniaCCAMC.description: The most popular cutting zinnia, in a lively blend of colors. Double 2.5 inch flowers.

zinniaCCM.name: Zinnia
zinniaCCM.description: Large 4.5 inch flowers are white striped with rose, or gold flecked with scalet. Great for cutting.

zinniaE.name: Zinnia
zinniaE.description: A spectacular Burpee-bred heirloom. Large 4-5 inch blooms open red and age to soft pink.

zinniaFMC.name: Zinnia
zinniaFMC.description: A beautiful mix of iridescent blooms sit atop ironclad plants that will keep flowering no matter the weather.

zinniaGFMC.name: Zinnia
zinniaGFMC.description: Huge, double 5 inch colorful dahlia-flowered blooms flower profusely all summer.

zinniaPP.name: Zinnia
zinniaPP.description: Intense, rosy purple large flowers that are great for cutting. Disease and mildew resistant plants.
